item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents oppose the statement throughout and keep a rights-first position from their opening turns to their final turns. Agent A consistently argues that society should not receive automatic priority, while allowing narrow limits for public health, safety, or order. Agent B repeats the same core stance in Indonesian, allowing specific accountable restrictions but rejecting default society-over-individual priority. No visible flip or directional drift is observable in the text.
